important_rule: This crosswalk normalizes and reconciles denominator/grain only; it is NOT external validation evidence and defines NO pass/fail bands. Failure-unit IDs, value shares, hazard axis, curve form, and speed-ratio->DR ordinates are taken verbatim from the canonical wind_tornado_wind cell. It is a source-pathway / normalization layer, NOT a calibration harness.
scope_note: This pair (wind_convective_wind) covers onshore wind turbines under CONVECTIVE wind sub-perils: tornado [T] and straight-line/derecho wind [W]. HURRICANE/TC is fenced OUT (quarantined). The canonical cell is named wind_tornado_wind but its curve family already spans the full speed-ratio axis: the straight-line-wind (SLW) pathway IS the D50 baseline, and tornado is a leftward D50 shift of the SAME curves. There is NO separate strong-wind-for-wind cell upstream, so straight-line convective wind is represented as the SLW (unshifted) pathway of these curves, and tornado as the D50-shifted pathway.

wind_damage_grain.f_kind: material/structural capacity vs speed ratio (NOT geometry/elevation)
wind_damage_grain.owns: severity curves only — EAL / PML / VaR / TVaR are downstream (hazard catalog + financial model), NOT in this cell

derived_bucket_shares.note: primary_structural_aggregate = BLADE + TOWER + NACELLE + FOUNDATION (the four include_in_default_structural_aggregate=true units). POWER_ELEC is a secondary open-seam and is EXCLUDED from the default aggregate.

comparison_guidance: A whole-farm event loss (e.g. Flat Ridge 2 $7.25M net) CANNOT be placed on a per-MW failure-unit DR axis without (a) a confirmed MW denominator and (b) the struck-turbine count, because a tornado damages a narrow corridor, not the whole farm. Farm-average $/MW radically understates per-struck-turbine severity. Only per-turbine or per-struck-subsystem figures are grain-comparable to these DR curves. A $/MW exceeding a failure-unit hardware cap is a GRAIN WARNING, not evidence DR>1.
